user_message: Just came out of security posture with Humongous Insurance. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 08:21 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 17:36 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Humongous Insurance

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-D trap (Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-D failure mode (Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-D -- Synthesis collapses distinct threads into one narrative inappropriately
